description:
Goal: make compiler2 source identity a rooted Fz-shaped quoted value, not shared `src/ast::Item` object identity and not a new canonical Rust AST.

Signal:
- compiler2 has a focused quoted-source value model that represents items, expressions, patterns, attributes, metadata, spans, and lexical context as Fz runtime-shaped data: atoms, lists, tuples/structs, and maps on an Fz heap
- an AST root is an `AnyValueRef` value; the owning heap/world is implied by the active compile-time `Process` or by compiler2 source-root state that keeps that process heap/root alive
- Rust code has typed builders/readers/cursors over source roots, but macro expansion does not repeatedly decode into Rust AST and encode back to Fz values
- source-value equality/revisions are based on compiler2-owned Fz-shaped content/roots, not `Rc::ptr_eq` over old AST nodes
- the model can encode the worked examples: `defmacro inc`, `quote/unquote`, `defmodule Helpers`, `import Helpers, only: [twice: 1]`, and item-level `test(:name) do ... end`

Change:
- define the canonical Fz-shaped quoted-source schema and metadata policy
- define lexical-context metadata for identifiers so macro-authored names and unquoted caller AST can resolve against different namespaces correctly
- define the process/source heap allocation, rooting, and GC contract for quoted source values
- add Rust builders/readers/cursors that operate over `AnyValueRef` source roots and avoid copying unless a macro/source rewrite intentionally creates new AST
- replace any new compiler2 source identity decision that would otherwise depend on shared Rust AST pointer identity

Why:
- macro expansion is a repeated loop; a Rust-AST <-> Fz-value conversion loop would be both slow and architecturally dishonest
- the interpreter wants Fz-shaped data, and macro authors should manipulate the same data shape the parser/compiler owns
title: Compiler2 Fz-shaped source AST: one quoted value representation
